name: InNat

aliases.name: InNat
aliases.comment:
A natural number somewhere within a minimum & maximum.

       ‚Üď minimum    ‚Üď maximum
    ‚®Į [‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď] ‚®Į ‚®Į ‚®Į...

Note: maximum >= minimum for every existing `InNat`:

    percent : InNat minimum Nat100

‚Üí `minimum <= Nat100`

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `InNat`.

    InNat.n nat3
        |> InNat.add between1And12 nat1 nat12
    --> of type InNat Nat4 (Nat15Plus a)
values.type: InNat.InNat addedMin addedMax -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N addedMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference min N.Type.To sumMin)) -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N addedMax N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference max N.Type.To sumMax)) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat sumMin sumMax

values.name: addN
values.comment:
Add a fixed `NNat` value.

    InNat.n nat70
        |> InNat.addN ( nat7, nat7 )
    --> is of type InNat Nat77 (Nat77Plus a)
values.type: ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N added N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference min N.Type.To sumMin)), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N added N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference max N.Type.To sumMax)) ) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat sumMin sumMax

values.name: atLeast
values.comment:
If the `InNat` is less than a number, return that number instead.

    InNat.n nat5
        |> InNat.atLeast nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> InNat 10

    InNat.n nat15
        |> InNat.lowerMin nat5
        |> InNat.atLeast nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> InNat 15
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N newMin N.Type.Is difference) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N oldMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference oldMinusNewMin N.Type.To newMin)) } -> InNat.InNat oldMin max -> InNat.InNat newMin max

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the `InNat` to at most a number.

    InNat.n nat5
        |> InNat.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> InNat 5

    InNat.n nat15
        |> InNat.lowerMin nat5
        |> InNat.atMost nat10 { min = nat5 }
    --> InNat 10

`min` ensures that that number is at least the minimum.
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N newMax N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To newMaxPlusA)) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference newRange N.Type.To newMax)) } -> InNat.InNat min oldMax -> InNat.InNat min newMaxPlusA

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `MinNat`. `div 0` is impossible.

    InNat.n nat7 |> InNat.div (MinNat.n nat3)
    --> InNat 2 of type InNat Nat0 (Nat7Plus a)
values.type: Internal.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus dMinMinus1) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat N.Nat.Type.Nat0 max

values.name: dropMax
values.comment:
Convert a `InNat` to a `MinNat`.

    squareAInNat =
        MinNat.dropMax >> MinNat.toPower (MinNat.n nat2)
values.type: InNat.InNat min max -> Internal.MinNat min

values.name: intInRange
values.comment:
A `InNat` from an `Int`, **clamped** between a `minimum` & `maximum`.

  - if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned
  - if the `Int > maximum`, `maximum` is returned

```
9 |> InNat.intInRange nat3 nat12 --> InNat 9

0 |> InNat.intInRange nat3 nat12 --> InNat 3

99 |> InNat.intInRange nat3 nat12 --> InNat 12
```

If you want to handle the cases `< minimum` & `> maximum` explicitly, use [`isIntInRange`][InNat#isIntInRange]
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference range N.Type.To max)) -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N max N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To maxPlusA)) -> Basics.Int -> InNat.InNat min maxPlusA

values.name: is
values.comment:
Compare the `InNat` to a `NNat`. Is it greater, less or equal to that `NNat`?

`min` ensures that the `NNat` is bigger than the minimum.

    justIfGreater4 =
        InNat.minIs nat0
            >> is ( nat4, nat4 )
                { min = nat0 }
                { greater = Just
                , less = \_ -> Nothing
                , equal = \() -> Nothing
                }


    justIfGreater4 (InNat.n nat5)
    --> Just (InNat 5)

    justIfGreater4 (InNat.n nat3)
    --> Nothing
values.type: ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N tried N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus greaterRange) N.Type.To max)), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N tried N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus triedPlusAMinus1))) ) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) N.Type.To tried)) } -> { equal : () -> result, less : InNat.InNat min triedPlusAMinus1 -> result, greater : InNat.InNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat2Plus triedMinus1) max -> result } -> InNat.InNat min max -> result

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `InNat`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `NNat` or

  - `less`?

```
vote : { a | age : InNat (Nat18Plus orOlder) max } -> Vote

tryToVote =
    InNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isAtLeast ( nat18, nat18 )
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = Nothing --ūüėď
            , equalOrGreater = Just << vote
            }
```
values.type: ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N tried N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinus1PlusA))), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N tried N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference atLeastRange N.Type.To max)) ) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) N.Type.To tried)) } -> { less : InNat.InNat min triedMinus1PlusA -> result, equalOrGreater : InNat.InNat tried max -> result } -> InNat.InNat min max -> result

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `InNat`

  - `equalOrLess` than a `NNat` or

  - `greater`?

```
goToU18Party : { a | age : InNat min Nat17 } -> Snacks

tryToGoToU18Party =
    InNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isAtMost ( nat17, nat17 )
            { min = nat0 }
            { equalOrLess = Just << goToU18Party
            , greater = Nothing
            }
```
values.type: ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N tried N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To triedPlusA)), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N tried N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus greaterRange) N.Type.To max)) ) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference atMostRange N.Type.To tried)) } -> { equalOrLess : InNat.InNat min triedPlusA -> result, greater : InNat.InNat tried max -> result } -> InNat.InNat min max -> result

values.name: isInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range `first` to `last`, is the `InNat`

  - `inRange`

  - `greater` than the `last` or

  - `less` than the `first`?

```
justIfBetween3And10 =
    InNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> InNat.isInRange
            { first = ( nat3, nat3 ), last = ( nat10, nat10 ) }
            { min : nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> Nothing
            , greater = \_ -> Nothing
            , inRange = Just
            }

justIfBetween3And10 (InNat.n nat9)
--> Just (InNat 9)

justIfBetween3And10 (InNat.n nat123)
--> Nothing
```
values.type: { first : ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N first N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference range N.Type.To last)), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N first N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus firstMinus1PlusA))) ), last : ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N last N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus greaterRange) N.Type.To max)), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N last N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To lastPlusA)) ) } -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) N.Type.To first)) } -> { inRange : InNat.InNat first lastPlusA -> result, less : InNat.InNat min firstMinus1PlusA -> result, greater : InNat.InNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus last) max -> result } -> InNat.InNat min max -> result

values.name: isIntInRange
values.comment:
An `Int` compared to a range from `first` to `last`.

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt =
        isIntInRange { first = nat1, last = nat100 }
            { less = Err "must be >= 1"
            , more = Err "must be <= 100"
            , inRange = Ok
            }

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt (InNat.n nat0)
    --> Err "must be >= 1"
values.type: { first : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N first N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference range N.Type.To last)), last : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N last N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To lastPlusA)) } -> { less : () -> result, greater : Internal.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus last) -> result, inRange : InNat.InNat first lastPlusA -> result } -> Basics.Int -> result

values.name: lowerMin
values.comment:
Set the minimum lower.

    [ InNat.n nat3, InNat.n nat4 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `InNat Nat3 ...`

    [ InNat.n nat3
    , InNat.n nat4 |> InNat.lowerMin nat3
    ]
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N lowerMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference currentMinusAlsoValidmin N.Type.To min)) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat lowerMin max

values.name: maxIs
values.comment:
Set the maximum higher.

You should design type annotations as general as possible.

    onlyAtMost18 : InNat min Nat18

    onlyAtMost18 between3And8 --fine

But once you implement `onlyAtMost18`, you might use the value in `onlyAtMost19`.

    onlyAtMost18 value =
        -- onlyAtMost19 value --error :(
        onlyAtMost19
            (value |> InNat.maxIs nat18 {- works :) -})
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N max N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To maxPlusA)) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat min maxPlusA

values.name: n
values.comment:
A `InNat` from an exact `NNat n`.

The minimum is `n`, the maximum `>= n`.
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N n N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To nPlusA)) -> InNat.InNat n nPlusA

values.name: random
values.comment: Generate a random `InNat` in a range.
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference range N.Type.To max)) -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N max N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To maxPlusA)) -> Random.Generator (InNat.InNat min maxPlusA)

values.name: range
values.comment:
`InNat`s from a first to a last value.

    from3To10 =
        InNat.range (nat3 |> InNat.n) (nat10 |> InNat.n)
values.type: InNat.InNat firstMin lastMin -> InNat.InNat lastMin lastMax -> List.List (InNat.InNat firstMin lastMax)

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after division. `remainderBy 0` is impossible.

    InNat.n nat7 |> InNat.remainderBy (MinNat.n nat3)
    --> InNat Nat0 (Nat7Plus a)
values.type: Internal.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus dMinMinus1) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat N.Nat.Type.Nat0 max

values.name: sub
values.comment:
Subtract a `InNat`.

    InNat.n nat6
        |> InNat.sub between1And5 nat1 nat5
    --> is of type InNat Nat1 (Nat5Plus a)
values.type: InNat.InNat subtractedMin subtractedMax -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N subtractedMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference differenceMax N.Type.To max)) -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N subtractedMax N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference differenceMin N.Type.To min)) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat differenceMin differenceMax

values.name: subN
values.comment:
Subtract a fixed `NNat` value.

    InNat.n nat7
        |> InNat.subN ( nat7, nat7 )
    --> InNat.n nat0
values.type: ( NNat.NNat (N.Type.N subtracted N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference differenceMin N.Type.To min)), NNat.NNat (N.Type.N subtracted N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference differenceMax N.Type.To max)) ) -> InNat.InNat min max -> InNat.InNat differenceMin differenceMax

values.name: toInt
values.comment: Convert the value in a `InNat` to an `Int`.
values.type: InNat.InNat min max -> Basics.Int

name: MinNat

aliases.name: MinNat
aliases.comment:
`NatFrom min` meaning a natural number which is at least `min`.

       ‚Üď minimum
    ‚®Į [‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď ‚úď...

Any natural number

    MinNat Nat0

A number, bigger than 4

    MinNat Nat5

values.name: abs
values.comment:
The absolute value of an `Int`, which is at least `Nat0`.

    MinNat.abs 16 --> MinNat 16

    MinNat.abs -4 --> MinNat 4

Really only use this if you want the absolute value.

    badLength list =
        List.length >> MinNat.abs

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ -> MinNat.addN nat1 >> MinNat.lowerMin nat0)
            (MinNat.n nat0)

If something like this isn't possible, use `[MinNat.intAtLeast][MinNat#intAtLeast] nat0`!
values.type: Basics.Int -> MinNat.MinNat N.Nat.Type.Nat0

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `MinNat`. The second argument is the minimum if the added `MinNat`.

    (nat5 |> MinNat.n)
        |> MinNat.add atLeast2 nat2
values.type: MinNat.MinNat addedMin -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N addedMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference min N.Type.To summin)) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat summin

values.name: addN
values.comment:
Add an exact `NNat`.

    (nat5 |> MinNat.n)
        |> MinNat.addN nat2
    --> MinNat 7
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N n N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference min N.Type.To minPlusN)) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat minPlusN

values.name: atMost
values.comment: **Cap** the `MinNat` to at most a upper limit.
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N max N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To maxPlusA)) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference range N.Type.To max)) } -> MinNat.MinNat min -> InNat.InNat min maxPlusA

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `MinNat`. `div 0` is impossible.

    MinNat.n nat7 |> MinNat.div (MinNat.n nat3)
    --> MinNat 2 of type MinNat Nat0
values.type: MinNat.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus minMinus1) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat N.Nat.Type.Nat0

values.name: intAtLeast
values.comment:
A `MinNat` from an `Int`; if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned.

    9 |> MinNat.intAtLeast nat3 --> MinNat 9

    0 |> MinNat.intAtLeast nat3 --> MinNat 3

You can also use this if you know an `Int` must be at least `minimum`.

But avoid it if you can do better, like

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ -> MinNat.addN nat1 >> MinNat.lowerMin nat0)
            (MinNat.n nat0)

If you want to handle the case `< minimum` yourself, use [`MinNat.isIntAtLeast`][MinNat#isIntAtLeast].
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N n N.Type.Is difference) -> Basics.Int -> MinNat.MinNat n

values.name: is
values.comment: Compared to an exact `NNat`.
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinus1) N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus aMinus1) N.Type.To (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinus1PlusA))) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference lessRange N.Type.To triedMinus1)) } -> { equal : () -> result, less : InNat.InNat min triedMinus1PlusA -> result, greater : MinNat.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat2Plus triedMinus1) -> result } -> MinNat.MinNat min -> result

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `MinNat`

  - `equalOrGreater` than a `NNat` or

  - `less`?

```
factorialHelp : MinNat min -> MinNat Nat1
factorialHelp =
    MinNat.lowerMin nat0
        >> MinNat.isAtLeast nat1
            { min = nat0 }
            { less = \_ -> MinNat.n nat1
            , equalOrGreater =
                \oneOrGreater ->
                    oneOrGreater
                        |> MinNat.mul
                            (factorialHelp
                                (oneOrGreater |> MinNat.subN nat1)
                            )
            }
```
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N triedMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus triedMinMinus1PlusA))) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus lessRange) N.Type.To triedMin)) } -> { less : InNat.InNat min triedMinMinus1PlusA -> result, equalOrGreater : MinNat.MinNat triedMin -> result } -> MinNat.MinNat min -> result

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `MinNat`

  - `equalOrLess` than a `NNat` or

  - `greater`?
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N atMostMin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference a N.Type.To atMostMinPlusA)) -> { min : NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference minToAtMostMin N.Type.To atMostMin)) } -> { equalOrLess : InNat.InNat min atMostMinPlusA -> result, greater : MinNat.MinNat atMostMin -> result } -> MinNat.MinNat min -> result

values.name: isIntAtLeast
values.comment:
If the `Int >= a minimum`, `Just the MinNat`, else `Nothing`.

    4 |> MinNat.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Nothing

    1234 |> MinNat.isIntAtLeast nat5 --> Just (NatIn 1234)
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N min N.Type.Is difference) -> Basics.Int -> Maybe.Maybe (MinNat.MinNat n)

values.name: lowerMin
values.comment: Set the minimum lower.
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N alsoValidmin N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference currentMinusAlsoValidmin N.Type.To min)) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat alsoValidmin

values.name: mul
values.comment:
Multiply by a `MinNat` >= 1.
We can't compute the highest possiblenew minimum,
we only know that if `a >= 1  ‚Üí  x * a >= x`

    five |> MinNat.mul two
    --> MinNat 10, but the type is MinNat Nat5

    two |> MinNat.mul five
    --> MinNat 10, but the type is MinNat Nat2

    two = nat2 |> MinNat.n

    five = nat5 |> MinNat.n
values.type: MinNat.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus multipliedMinMinus1) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat min

values.name: n
values.comment:
Convert an exact `NNat n` to a `MinNat n (n + a)`.

    MinNat.n nat4
    --> is of type MinNat Nat4 (Nat4Plus a)
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N n N.Type.Is difference) -> MinNat.MinNat n

values.name: range
values.comment: `MinNat`s from a first `InNat` to a last `MinNat`.
values.type: InNat.InNat firstMin lastMin -> MinNat.MinNat lastMin -> List.List (MinNat.MinNat firstMin)

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after division. `remainderBy 0` is impossible.

    MinNat.n nat7 |> MinNat.remainderBy (MinNat.n nat3)
    --> MinNat Nat0
values.type: MinNat.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus dMinMinus1) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat N.Nat.Type.Nat0

values.name: subIn
values.comment:
Subtract a `InNat`. The second argument is the maximum if the subtracted `InNat`.

    (nat5 |> MinNat.n)
        |> MinNat.subIn inNat0To5 itsMaximum
values.type: InNat.InNat subtractedMin subtractedMax -> NNat.NNat (N.Type.N subtractedMax N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference differenceMin N.Type.To min)) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat differenceMin

values.name: subN
values.comment:
Subtract an exact `NNat`.

    (nat7 |> MinNat.n)
        |> MinNat.subN nat2
    --> MinNat 5
values.type: NNat.NNat (N.Type.N n N.Type.Is (N.Type.Difference minMinusN N.Type.To min)) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat minMinusN

values.name: theGreater
values.comment:
The greater of 2 `MinNat`s. Works just like [Basics.max][Basics#max].

    MinNat.theGreater
        (nat3 |> MinNat.n)
        (nat4 |> MinNat.n |> MinNat.lowerMin nat3)
    --> MinNat 4
values.type: MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat min

values.name: theSmaller
values.comment:
The smaller of 2 `MinNat`s. Works just like [Basics.min][Basics#min].

    MinNat.theSmaller
        (nat3 |> MinNat.n)
        (nat4 |> MinNat.n |> MinNat.lowerMin nat3)
    --> MinNat 3
values.type: MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat min

values.name: toInt
values.comment:
Convert a `MinNat` to an `Int`.

    MinNat.n nat4 |> MinNat.toInt --> 4

    compare a b =
        compare (MinNat.toInt a) (MinNat.toInt b)
values.type: MinNat.MinNat min -> Basics.Int

values.name: toPower
values.comment:
The `MinNat ^ a MinNat`.
We can't compute the highest possible new minimum,
we only know that if `a >= 1  ‚Üí  x ^ a >= x`

    five |> MinNat.toPower two
    --> MinNat 25, but the type is MinNat Nat5

    two |> MinNat.mul five
    --> MinNat 25, but the type is MinNat Nat2

    two = nat2 |> MinNat.n

    five = nat5 |> MinNat.n
values.type: MinNat.MinNat (N.Nat.Type.Nat1Plus minMinus1) -> MinNat.MinNat min -> MinNat.MinNat min
